# Which venue moves first? Cross-venue price discovery

*Auto-generated by `scripts/leadlag_note.py` on 2026-07-21 19:47 UTC from `data/history/` (same recorded snapshots as the edge-persistence note).*

## Method

Mids move sparsely at snapshot cadence, so raw return cross-correlation is dominated by zeros. Instead, move-conditional analysis: a *move* is a mid change of at least 0.2500¢ between consecutive co-observed snapshots; for every venue-A move we look ahead up to 10 snapshots for venue B's first move and classify it follow (same direction), oppose, or unanswered. Same-tick moves land in a `simultaneous` bucket — sub-cadence leadership is unobservable and never attributed. Recording gaps split sessions; nothing is measured across a gap. The test statistic is an exact two-sided binomial on follows vs opposes against p = 0.5.

## Per-pair results (0.25¢ threshold)

Columns: moves | simultaneous | follow/oppose/unanswered | follow rate (of directional answers) | median follow lag | binomial p

|---|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|

| | pm→kalshi | 7 | 0 | 2/2/3 | 50% | 456s | 1.000 |

| | pm→kalshi | 131 | 47 | 37/28/19 | 57% | 49s | 0.321 |

## Pooled across all pairs

| leader | moves | simul | F/O/U | follow | med lag | p |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| kalshi→pm | 903 | 75 | 67/59/702 | 53% | 65s | 0.533 |
| pm→kalshi | 328 | 75 | 70/53/130 | 57% | 49s | 0.149 |

## Reading

- No clear leader at this cadence: follow rates 53% (K→PM) vs 57% (PM→K). Much of the action is in the `simultaneous` bucket — leadership, if any, plays out faster than the snapshot cadence.
- 150 same-tick move pairs vs 137 cross-tick follows: the cadence floor is doing real censoring; the BYOK websocket feed (sub-second) will sharpen this materially.
- Unanswered moves are informative too: a venue whose moves the other never validates is either discovering price alone or drifting on noise/one-sided flow.

## Threshold sensitivity (pooled)

| threshold | kalshi→pm follow (p) | pm→kalshi follow (p) |
|---:|---:|---:|
| 0.2500¢ | 53% (p=0.533, n=126) | 57% (p=0.149, n=123) |
| 0.100¢ | 51% (p=0.803, n=144) | 54% (p=0.246, n=268) |
| 0.500¢ | 54% (p=0.547, n=99) | 52% (p=0.901, n=64) |

## Caveats

- Both venues are sampled at the same instant per snapshot, so there is no cross-venue clock skew inside a tick — but anything faster than the cadence is invisible (see `simultaneous`).
- Kalshi mids move on a cent grid; Polymarket's finer grid produces more small moves — the threshold sensitivity table bounds how much that asymmetry matters.
- Events are pooled across pairs and days and treated as independent; clustered news moves violate that mildly.
- Sparse-sample study: rates are honest but confidence grows with the recorder (running continuously since 2026-07-21).

*Upcoming: the 2026-07-29 Fed decision resolves the densest pair mid-recording — an event-study section lands here after it.*
